Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Data Analysis: Events 1-3 (08:00:00 - 08:00:10): Temp: ~20C Pressure: 1 bar Vibration: 0 Hz Flow: 0 Tool Pos: 0 Energy: Low (~0.4-0.5 kWh) Interpretation: Idle state or Start-up / Standby. Events 4-6 (08:00:15 - 08:00:25): Temp: Rising rapidly (20 -> 50 -> 55 -> 58C). Pressure: Slight increase (1 -> 1.5 bar). Vibration: Moderate increase (0 -> 30 -> 35 -> 38 Hz). Flow: Constant low flow (5 units/s). Tool Position: Moving (0 -> 5 -> 7 -> 10 mm). Energy: Increasing significantly (0.45 -> 2.0 -> 2.5 -> 3.0 kWh). Events 7-8 (08:00:30 - 08:00:35): Temp: Stabilized at 40C. Pressure: Increased (2 bar). Vibration: Dropped (10 Hz). Flow: Low (2 units/s). Tool Position: Static (15 mm). Energy: Moderate/Stable (2.0 kWh). Event 9 (08:00:40): Temp: Spikes high (80C). Pressure: Stable (2 bar). Vibration: High spike (50 Hz). Flow: 0. Tool Position: Static (15 mm). Energy: Peak consumption (5.0 kWh). Event 10 (08:00:45): Temp: Drops fast (30C). Pressure: Drops to baseline (1 bar). Vibration: Minimal (1 Hz). Flow: 0. Energy: Low (1.0 kWh). Events 11-12 (08:00:50 - 08:00:55): Temp: Room temp (25C). Pressure: Baseline (1 bar). Vibration: Very low (5 Hz). Flow: Resumed (1 unit/s). Tool Position: Moving again (20 mm). Energy: Low/Moderate (1.5-1.6 kWh). Events 13-15 (08:01:00 - 08:01:10): All values return to baseline.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interpretation: This looks like a mechanical process involving contact and material removal or shaping. The rising temperature and vibration suggest friction. This fits Cutting or Machining. Interpretation: A pause or a holding phase. Could be Positioning or Clamping before the next intense step. Interpretation: High heat, high energy, no material flow, tool static. This is characteristic of Welding or intense Pressing/Forging step, but Welding is a strong candidate. Interpretation: Cool down / Transition. Interpretation: Post-processing. Moving the part out. Ejection or Unloading or Quality Check. Interpretation: Return to Idle state.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesizing the Phases: 1. Phase 1 (Events 1-3): Idle / Standby. 2. Phase 2 (Events 4-6): Cutting/Milling. 3. Phase 3 (Events 7-8): Positioning/Preparation. 4. Phase 4 (Event 9): Welding/Joining. 5. Phase 5 (Event 10): Cooling. 6. Phase 6 (Events 11-12): Inspection/Ejection. 7. Phase 7 (Events 13-15): Idle.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Refine the labels based on standard manufacturing archetypes: Group 1 (Events 1-3 & 13-15): System Idle / Standby. Group 2 (Events 4-6): Cutting Metal. Reasoning: Rapid increase in Temperature and Vibration indicates physical friction. Material Flow is present. Tool is moving. Group 3 (Events 7-8): Assembling Parts (or Preparation/Clamping). Reasoning: Pressure increases (clamping force), Tool stops moving (holding), Flow slows. Group 4 (Event 9): Welding. Reasoning: Extreme Temperature spike (80C), massive Energy draw (5kWh), high Vibration (50Hz). Group 5 (Events 11-12): Quality Inspection / Ejection. Reasoning: Process is done. Values are normalizing. There is slight movement and low flow.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Drafting the Output Structure. Lets double check Event 7-8. Temp drops from 58 to 40. Vibration drops from 38 to 10. Pressure goes up to 2. This looks like clamping – pressing parts together firmly before welding. Final check of Event 10. It sits right after the big spike. Its a cooling off point. Refined Groupings: Activity 1: System Idle (Events 1-3, 13-15). Activity 2: Cutting Metal (Events 4-6). Activity 3: Part Assembly/Clamping (Events 7-8). Activity 4: Welding (Event 9). Activity 5: Quality Control & Unloading (Events 10-12).
